hadiths.chapter: 117. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
hadiths.en:
Hanan ibn Sudayr narrated:  
I was sitting with Abu Abdullah {a.s} when a man asked Imam {a.s}, "Can one enter Ihram 
wearing a garment that contains silk?"  
Imam {a.s} called for a striped lower garment ( izar furqubi ) and said: "I enter Ihram in 
this, and it contains silk."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Qurb al-Isnad, Part 1, p. 99 • Bihar Al-Anwar, Vol. 96, p. 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 117, H. 2603

reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND

hadiths.chapter: 117. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
hadiths.ar:
2605  -  وَّ ق ِى  رِوَّإي َّه ِ مُّعَّاوِي َّه َّ ن ْن ِ عَّم َّارٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال لز َّج ُّلُّ ق ِى  إَّلي  َّوْب ِ 
إَّلْمُّعْلَّمِ وَّ ي َّزْكُّهُّ إ َّجَّث  ُّ إ ِلَّى  َّ إ ِذ َّإ ق َّذَّرَّ عَّلَّى ع َّي ْزِةِ.
hadiths.en:
It was narrated from Al-Halabi, who said:  
I asked Imam {a.s} about a man entering Ihram wearing a garment with an embroidered 
border.  
Imam {a.s} said: "There is no harm in it."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 571  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 117, H. 2604

reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND

hadiths.chapter: 117. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
hadiths.en:
In a narration from Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar, from Abu Abdullah {a.s}, he said:  
"There is no harm if a man enters Ihram wearing an embroidered garment, but avoiding 
it is more beloved to me if he has the means to wear something else."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol. 5, p. 71 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 571 
• Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 12, p. 479  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 117, H. 2605

hadiths.chapter: 117. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
hadiths.ar:
2607  -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّهُّ إَّلْحُّسَّي ْن ُّ ن ْن ُّ إ َّن ِى  إَّلْعَّلاَّءِ : عَّن ِ إَّلي  َّوْب ِ لِلْمُّحْزِمِ ي ُّصِي ن ُّهُّ إَّلز  َّعْق َّزَّإن ُّ ي ُّم َّ ي ُّع ْسَّلُّ ق َّق َّا ل
ذ َّهَّث َّ رِي حُّهُّ وَّ لَّوْ كَّان َّ مَّصْن ُّوع اً كُّل ُّهُّ إ ِذ َّإ ص ُّزِب َّ إ ِلَّى إَّلْن َّي َّاض ِ وَّ ع ُّسِلَّ ق َّلاَّ ن َّا ْ س
hadiths.en:
Laith al -Muradi asked Imam {a.s} about a man entering Ihram in an 
embroidered garment.  
Imam {a.s} said: "Yes, it is permissible. What is disliked is a fully patterned garment 
(mulham)."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al -Usul al-Sitta ‘Ashar (Dar Al-Hadith Edition) Vol. 1, p. 
254 • Mustadrak Al-Wasail, Vol. 9, p. 220  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 117, H. 2606

reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND

hadiths.chapter: 117. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
hadiths.ar:
2608  -  ورَّوَّى إَّلْق َّاسِمُّ ن ْن ُّ مُّحَّم َّذٍ إَّلْح َّوْهَّزِى  ُّ عَّن ْ عَّلِى ِّ ن ْن ِ إ َّن ِى  جَّمْز َّة َّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّل اَّمُّ ق َّال 
إُّص ْطُّز َّ إَّلْمُّحْزِمُّ إ ِلَّى إ َّن ْ ي َّلْن َّسَّ ق َّن َّاءً مِن ْ ن َّزْذٍ وَّ لأَّ ي َّح ِذُّ ي َّوْن اً ع َّي ْزَّةُّ ق َّلْي َّلْن َّسْهُّ مَّق ْ لُّون اً وَّ لأَّ ي ُّذْج ِلْ ي َّذَّي ْهِ ق ِى  ي َّذَّى ِ إَّلْق َّن َّاءِ.
hadiths.en:
Al-Husayn ibn Abi al-‘Ala’ asked Imam {a.s} about a garment for a Muhrim that 
has been stained with saffron and then washed.   
Imam {a.s} said: "There is no harm in it if the scent has been removed. Even if it has been 
entirely dyed, as long as it leans toward whiteness and has been washed, there is no 
harm in it."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 117, H. 2607
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND

hadiths.chapter: 117. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
hadiths.ar:
2609 - وَّ رُّوِى َّ عَّن ِ إَّلْكَّاهِلِى ِّ ق َّالَّ : سَّا َّلَّهُّ رَّج ُّل  وَّ إ َّن َّا جَّاص ِز  عَّن ِ إَّلي  َّوْب ِ ي َّكُّون ُّ مَّصْن ُّوع اً ن ِالْعُّصْق ُّزِ ي ُّم  ي ُّع ْسَّلُّ إ َّلْن َّسُّهُّ وَّ 
إ َّن َّا مُّحْزِم  ق َّق َّال اسُّ.
hadiths.en:
Al-Qasim ibn Muhammad al-Jawhari narrated from Ali ibn Abi Hamzah, from 
Abu Abdullah {a.s}, who said:  
"If a Muhrim is compelled to wear a cloak ( qaba’) made of striped cloth and cannot 
find any other garment, he may wear it inside out and should not insert his arms into 
the sleeves of the cloak."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol. 5, p. 70 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 576 
• Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 12, p. 486 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 12, p. 487 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 12, p. 487  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 117, H. 2608

reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND

hadiths.chapter: 117. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
hadiths.ar:
2610 - وَّ سَّا َّلَّهُّ إ ِسْمَّاعِي لُّ ن ْن ُّ إَّلْق َّص ْلِ : عَّن ِ إَّلْمُّحْزِمِ إ َّ ي َّلْن َّسُّ إَّلي  َّوْب َّ ق َّذْ إ َّصَّان َّهُّ إَّلطِّي ث ُّ ق َّق َّال هَّث َّ رِي حُّ إَّلطِّي ث ِ  
ق َّلْي َّلْن َّسْهُّ.
hadiths.en:
It was narrated from al -Kahili, who said:  A man asked Imam {a.s} in my 
presence about a garment dyed with safflower (‘usfur) that has been washed.  
"Can I wear it while in the state of Ihram?"   
Imam {a.s} replied: "Yes, safflower is not considered a fragrance. However, I dislike for 
you to wear something that makes you stand out among people."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 117, H. 2609

hadiths.chapter: 117. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
hadiths.ar:
2611   -  وَّ رُّوِى َّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  إَّلْحَّسَّن ِ إَّلن  َّهْذِى ِّ ق َّالَّ : سَّا َّلَّ سَّعِي ذ  إَّلْأ َّعْزَّح ُّ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ وَّ إ َّن َّا عِن ْذَّةُّ عَّن ِ إَّلْح َّمِي صَّه ِ 
سَّذَّإهَّا إ ِن ْزِي سَّم  وَّ لَّحْمَّي ُّهَّا مِزْعِز  َّى ق َّق َّال ا
hadiths.en:
Isma'il ibn al -Fadl asked Imam {a.s} about whether a Muhrim can wear a 
garment that has been affected by perfume.   
Imam {a.s} said: "If the scent of the perfume has disappeared, then he may wear it."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 117, H. 2610

hadiths.chapter: 117. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
hadiths.ar:
2612  -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّ جَّم َّاذُّ ن ْن ُّ عُّي ْمَّان َّ إ َّن َّا عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : عَّن ْ ج َّلُّوق ِ إَّلْكَّعْن َّه ِ وَّ ج َّلُّوق ِ إَّلْق َّن ْزِ  ي َّكُّون ُّ ق ِى  ي َّوْب ِ 
إَّلْأ ِجْزَّإمِ ق َّق َّال
hadiths.en:
It was narrated from Abu al-Hasan al-Nahdi, who said:  
Sa'id al -A'raj asked Abu Abdullah {a.s} in my presence about a garment ( khamisah) 
whose warp was made of silk (ibrīsīm) and its weft was made of goat wool (mir‘izza).  
Imam {a.s} said: "There is no harm in entering Ihram in it. What is disliked is the garment 
made entirely of silk."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 117, H. 2611
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND

hadiths.chapter: 117. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
hadiths.en:
Hammad ibn Uthman asked Abu Abdullah {a.s} about the khaluq of the Ka'bah 
and the khaluq of the grave that might be present on the Ihram garment.   
Imam {a.s} said: "There is no harm in them; both are pure."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 117, H. 2612

reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ١١٧، ح ٢٦١٢

hadiths.chapter: 117. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
hadiths.ar:
2614  - وَّ رَّوَّى إَّلْحَّلَّن ِى  ُّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : ق ِى  إَّلْمُّحْزِمِ ي َّلْن َّسُّ إَّلط َّي ْلَّسَّان َّ إَّلْمُّز َّر رَّ ق َّال 
عَّلِى ٍّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم طَّي ْلَّسَّان اً جَّي  َّى ي َّحُّل َّ إ َّر ْرَّإرَّة 
ق َّا َّم َّا إَّلْق َّق ِي هُّ ق َّلاَّ ن َّا ْسَّ إ َّن ْ ي َّلْن َّسَّه
hadiths.en:
Sama'ah asked Imam {a.s} about a man whose garment is stained with the 
saffron of the Ka'bah while he is in the state of Ihram.   
Imam {a.s} said: "There is no harm in it; it is pure, so do not avoid it if it touches you."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 576  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 117, H. 2613

hadiths.chapter: 117. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
hadiths.en:
Al-Halabi narrated from Abu Abdullah {a.s} regarding a Muhrim wearing a 
buttoned taylasan (cloak).  
Imam {a.s} said: "Yes, as stated in the book of Imam Ali {a.s}: 'Do not wear a taylasan until 
you undo its buttons.' He also said: 'It was disliked only out of concern that an ignorant 
person might button it while wearing it. As for someone knowledgeable, there is no 
harm in wearing it.'"  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 117, H. 2614

reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ١١٧، ح ٢٦١٤

hadiths.chapter: 117. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
hadiths.ar:
2616  -  وَّ رَّوَّى مُّحَّم َّذُّ ن ْن ُّ مُّسْلِمٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : ق ِى إَّلْمُّحْزِمِ ي َّلْن َّسُّ إَّلْح ُّف  َّ إ ِذ َّإ لَّمْ ي َّكُّن ْ لَّهُّ ن َّعْل  ق َّالَّ  
«هْزَّةُّ لِن َّاطِن ِهِ
hadiths.en:
Rifa’ah ibn Musa asked Imam {a.s} about a Muhrim wearing socks (jawrabayn).  
Imam {a.s} said: "Yes, and also leather slippers ( khuffayn) if he is compelled to wear 
them."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 117, H. 2615
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ١١٧، ح ٢٦١٥

hadiths.chapter: 117. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
hadiths.ar:
2617  - وَّ رَّوَّى مُّعَّاوِي َّه ُّ ن ْن ُّ عَّم َّارٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال ر  وَّ إ َّن ْث َّ مُّحْزِم  إ ِلأ َّ إ َّن ْ 
ي َّن ْكُّسَّهُّ وَّ لأَّ ي َّوْن اً ي َّذَّر َّعُّهُّ وَّ لأَّ سَّزَّإوِي لَّ إ ِلأ َّ إ َّن ْ لأَّ ي َّكُّون َّ لَّك َّ إ ِر َّإر  وَّ لأَّ ج ُّق  َّي ْن ِ إ ِلأ َّ إ َّن ْ ي َّكُّون َّ لَّك َّ ن َّعْلاَّن ِ.
hadiths.en:
Muhammad ibn Muslim narrated from Abu Ja'far {a.s} regarding a Muhrim 
wearing leather slippers (khuff) if he does not have sandals.  
Imam {a.s} said: "Yes, but he must cut open the top of the foot area. A Muhrim may also 
wear a cloak (qaba’) if he does not have a cloak-like garment (rida’), but he should wear 
it inside out."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 578  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 117, H. 2616
reference.chapter: CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
reference.location: Volume 2 › CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ١١٧، ح ٢٦١٦

hadiths.chapter: 117. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
hadiths.ar:
2618  -  وَّ رَّوَّى ر ُّرَّإرَّة ُّ عَّن ْ إ َّجَّذِهِمَّا عَّلَّي ْهِمَّا إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّالَّ : سَّا َّلْي ُّهُّ عَّم َّا ي ُّكْزَّةُّ لِلْمُّحْزِمِ إ َّن ْ ي َّلْن َّسَّهُّ ق ق َّال 
إ ِلأ َّ ي َّوْن اً وَّإجِذإً ي َّي َّذَّر َّعُّه
hadiths.en:
Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar narrated from Abu Abdullah {a.s}, who said:  
"Do not wear a garment with buttons while you are in the state of Ihram unless you 
wear it inside out. Do not wear a garment that is worn like a tunic ( tadarru’), nor 
trousers ( sarawil), unless you have no loincloth ( izar). Do not wear leather slippers 
(khuffayn) unless you have no sandals (na'lan)."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 12, p. 473  
﴿HADITH ﴾

hadiths.chapter: 117. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
hadiths.ar:
2619   -  وَّ رَّوَّى مُّعَّاوِي َّه ُّ ن ْن ُّ عَّم َّارٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال مُّحْزِمُّ ي ِي َّان َّهُّ وَّ لَّكِن ْ إ ِذ َّإ  
ذَّج َّلَّ مَّك َّه َّ لَّن ِسَّ ي َّوْن َّى ْ إ ِجْزَّإمِهِ إَّلل َّذ َّي ْن ِ إ َّجْزَّمَّ ق ِي هِمَّا وَّ كُّزِةَّ إ َّن ْ ي َّن ِي عَّهُّمَّا 
وق ذ روي ث  رج صه  ق ى  ن ي عهما.
hadiths.en:
Zurara narrated from one of the Imams {a.s}, who said: I asked Imam {a.s} about 
what is disliked for a Muhrim to wear.   
Imam {a.s} said: "He may wear any garment except a single garment worn like a tunic 
(tadarru’)."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 578 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 12, p. 475 
• Al-Fusul al-Muhimmah, Part 2, p. 195  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 117, H. 2618

hadiths.chapter: 117. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
hadiths.ar:
2620  -  وَّ رَّوَّى إ َّن ُّو ن َّصِي زٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّالَّ سَّمِعْي ُّهُّ ي َّق ُّول  عَّلَّى إَّلْق ِزَّإس ِ  
إَّلْأ َّصْق َّزِ إ َّوِ إَّلْمِزْق َّق َّه ِ
hadiths.en:
Mu'awiyah ibn Ammar narrated from Abu Abdullah {a.s}, who said:  
"There is no harm if a Muhrim changes his clothes. However, when he enters Makkah, 
he should wear the two garments of Ihram that he entered Ihram with. It is disliked to 
sell them."  
It has also been narrated that there is a concession allowing their sale.   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 341 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, Vol. 5, p. 71 • 
Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 12, p. 363  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 117, H. 2619

hadiths.chapter: 117. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
hadiths.en:
Abu Basir narrated from Abu Ja'far {a.s}, who said:  
"I dislike for a Muhrim to sleep on a yellow mattress or a yellow cushion."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Usul al-Sitta ‘Ashar (Dar Shabestari Edition) Vol. 1, p. 
28 • Al-Usul al-Sitta ‘Ashar (Dar Al-Hadith Edition) Vol. 1, p. 161 • Al-Kafi, Vol. 4, p. 355 • Tahdhib Al-Ahkam, 
Vol. 5, p. 68 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 621 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 621 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 12, p. 457 • Wasa’il Al-
Shi’a, Vol. 12, p. 457 • Mustadrak Al-Wasail, Vol. 9, p. 212  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 117, H. 2620

hadiths.chapter: 117. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
hadiths.ar: 2622 - وَّ رَّوَّى عَّن ْذُّ إَّلل َّهِ ن ْن سِن َّان ٍ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال
hadiths.en:
Abdul Rahman ibn al-Hajjaj asked Abu al-Hasan {a.s} about a Muhrim wearing 
khazz (a type of fabric).  
Imam {a.s} said: "There is no harm in it."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2  
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 2, Ch. 117, H. 2621

hadiths.chapter: 117. CHAPTER ON WHAT IS PERMISSIBLE AND
hadiths.ar:
2623   -  وَّ رَّوَّى مُّحَّم َّذُّ ن ْن ُّ مُّسْلِمٍ عَّن ْ إ َّجَّذِهِمَّا عَّلَّي ْهِمَّا إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّالَّ : سَّا َّلْي ُّهُّ عَّن ِ إَّلْمُّحْزِمِ إ ِذ َّإ إِجْي َّاح َّ إ ِلَّى ص ُّزُّوب ٍ مِن َّ  
إَّلي ِّي َّاب ِ مُّح ْي َّلِق َّه ٍ ق َّق َّالَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم
hadiths.en:
Abdullah ibn Sinan narrated from Abu Abdullah {a.s}, who said:  
"If a Muhrim fears for his safety, he may wear weapons."   
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 2 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 12, p. 580 • Wasa’il Al-Shi’a, Vol. 12, p. 504  
﴿HADITH ﴾
